title: Module 1
content:
Fundamental Of Tissue Engineering: Fundamentals Of Stem Cell Tissue
Engineering; Growth Factors; Extracellular Matrix: Structure, Function And
Tissue Engineering Application; Mechanical Forces On Cells; Cell
Adhesion; Cell Migration. Tissue Engineering Enabling Technologies:
Polymer Scaffold For Tissue Engineering Applications; Biomimetic
Materials; Nanocomposite Scaffolds Tissue Engineering; Bioreactors;
Regulatory Issues In Tissue Engineering.

title: Module 2
content:
Tissue Engineering Application: Bioengineering Of Human Skin
Substitute; Nerve Tissue Engineering; Musculoskeletal Tissue Engineering;
Bone
Tissue
Engineering;
Cartilage
Tissue
Engineering;
Temporomandibular
Tissue
Engineering;
Smooth
Muscle
Tissue
Engineering; Oesophagus Tissue Engineering. Vascular Graft Tissue
Engineering Cardiac Tissue Engineering; Heart Valve Tissue Engineering;
Urologic Organ Tissue Engineering; Hepatic Tissue Engineering; Renal
Tissue
Engineering;
Dental
Tissue
Engineering;
Tracheal
Tissue
Engineering.

title: Module 3
content:
In vitro Biofabrications of tissues and organs: Scaffold free tissue
engineering, classification of present scaffold free system, in vitro self-
produced ECM rich scaffold free constructs, the rotating wall vessel
Bioreactor system. Aggregation/Spheroid based approaches: Preparation
of multicellular spheroids, Molding MCS, Bioprinting

title: Module 4
content:
Biofabrication of 3D tissue scaffolds: Conventional techniques for 3D
scaffolds, Advanced techniques for 3D scaffolds. Applications of 3D tissue
scaffolds: Nanofibrous scaffolds, Hydrogel scaffolds. Fabrication of
Microscale
Hydrogels:
Photolithography,
Soft
photolithography,
Bioprinting, Microfluidics. Laser assisted Bioprinting: Workstation
requirement, Droplet ejection mechanism, The bio ink as a scaffold,
Biopapers
